text:
One AI video isn't one prompt.

It's a process.

Start with still shots. Piece the frames together — frame one to frame two, add motion, get a clip. Do it five times.

Then stitch the clips into one continuous story in the editor.

That's exactly how we built this on Tech Thursday. No magic. Just the build, in the open.

Watch the full breakdown.

visual.library_hint: vertical screen-recording walking through the AI video build: still shots to frame pairs to five clips, stitched together in the editor

text:
Anthropic kept a model called Mythos under wraps — too powerful to ship.

This week: Fable 5, the first "Mythos-class" release. Agentic coding around 80%.

Knowing what dropped is step one. Building with it is the point.

visual.library_hint: minimal type card on deep black, electric blue accent, Fable 5 / Mythos-class benchmark motif

text:
We're building the thing we keep talking about.

A content engine that drafts tomorrow's posts, lines up the visuals, and then waits for a human to say yes.

The shift everyone's feeling is real: AI is moving from one prompt to agents that keep working while you sleep.

But autonomy without judgment is just noise. So nothing here publishes until a person approves it.

That's the eighth layer — people deciding what the tools actually mean.

Comment "build" if you want the breakdown.

The image should feel like a premium technology documentary and creative studio brand. livestream studio, microphone, laptop, coding dashboard, creator speaking live, professional broadcast setup, blue-black cinematic lighting, real build session. text:
ChatGPT just passed a billion monthly users.

A billion people consuming the AI era.

The real question: how many are building in it?

Pick one tool this week. Make something. Shape what comes next.

visual.library_hint: minimal quote card on deep black with soft white type and a single electric blue accent, a billion consuming

text:
midnight in the focus room.

soft blue glow, a warm lamp, the city humming outside.

beats to code, study, and build to — running 24/7.

settle in. open your laptop. build the future with us.

▶ layer8culture.io/lofi

visual.higgsfield_prompt: Create a cinematic anime-inspired, illustrated Layer8Culture LoFi scene (not photorealistic) — a densely detailed, lived-in environment. A Black coder or creator at work, lit by a warm desk lamp against a deep blue night. A futuristic African city skyline glows through a large window. A neon 'LAYER8 CULTURE' sign on the wall, and the standalone 8 motif present somewhere in the scene (on a hoodie, a mug, or the wall). Bookshelves filled with coding books and West African-inspired objects, plants throughout, ambient blue and gold lighting, warm and immersive, with a loopable composition. The image should feel like a calm Afrofuturistic focus room for builders. Scene: deep midnight, the creator leans back from the keyboard cradling a warm mug with steam rising, soft code glow on the screen, the skyline twinkling through the window; vertical 9:16 framing with the desk in the foreground and the city above. text:
The conversation is over.

AI isn't answering your questions anymore.
It's doing the work.

Claude Fable 5 just dropped. Every major lab is shipping the same message right now:
chat is the baseline.
Agents that plan, execute, fix, and loop — that's the new floor.

This isn't about prompting smarter.
It's about knowing how to build with tools that act.

AI fluency isn't reading about it.
It's showing up where the builds happen.

That's why we do this. 🔵

visual.openai_prompt: Create a cinematic Layer8Culture visual in a deep black environment with electric blue accent lighting, premium creator-tech atmosphere, late-night builder energy, documentary realism, human-centered AI themes, Black tech futurism, minimal composition, strong negative space, warm practical lighting, and high contrast. The scene is a built-out premium broadcast studio (not a makeshift setup): a blank neon sign panel on the wall (an unlit, lettering-free glowing panel — do NOT render any text or letters; the wordmark is composited in afterward), the standalone 8 motif on a hoodie or mug, a branded mic flag, a stream deck on a dark wood desk, and a bookshelf washed in blue accent lighting. A confident Black creator/host is present and engaged — direct-to-camera or actively presenting, never anonymous side profiles. The image should feel like a premium technology documentary and creative studio brand. Scene: a confident Black creator at the broadcast desk leaning forward in focused intensity, a large widescreen monitor behind them displaying an abstract multi-node agentic AI workflow diagram in electric blue — connected task boxes forming a loop, late-night studio atmosphere, deep blue-black background, no rendered text on any screen. Avoid generic startup office, bright corporate lighting, cheap gamer RGB, cluttered composition, cartoon props, excessive sci-fi armor, robots, fake hologram overload, random neon colors, copyrighted franchise references, garbled screen text, random figurines, tripod-mounted monitors, makeshift setups, rendered text, signage lettering, and garbled typography.

text:
We built a content engine that runs on AI.

Posts, image prompts, scheduling — all generated by Copilot CLI.
No manual guessing. No template drag.
Just a pipeline that ships every day.

This is the build.

Not because it's finished.
Because it's running.
And we're showing every step.

The future belongs to builders. 🔵

text:
One platform. Every major model.

On Tech Thursday we were live inside HiggsField — Claude, GPT, Google VO, Grok, all of them in one place.
Running image models. Video models. Comparing outputs in real time.

This is how you figure out what tools actually work for you.
Not reviews. Not Reddit threads.
Live experimentation, in the lab, on stream.

You don't need to pick a side.
You need to learn the field.

Watch the full session → link in bio. 👇
#TechThursday

text:
Be honest.

How many 30-second clips have you watched about AI this week?

On Tech Thursday, someone said it perfectly:
“You can watch 20 clips on the same subject and feel like an expert.
But you just spent 20 minutes on it.”

Depth is the move.
Not more content. More time in the tools.

When did you actually sit down and build something?

Drop it below. 👇
